message.content:
Search the web for pomegranate plant disease image datasets that are publicly available. I need:

1. Datasets with pomegranate disease photos from DIFFERENT sources than the Kaggle "sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset" (which has Alternaria, Anthracnose, Bacterial Blight, Cercospora, Healthy classes)
2. Datasets that include pomegranate diseases NOT in our current 5-class model — things like Phytophthora, heart rot, fruit borer damage, leaf curl, wilt, etc.
3. Any benchmark datasets used in published papers on pomegranate disease detection

Search Kaggle, HuggingFace datasets, PlantVillage, Roboflow Universe, and any other public sources.

Return: dataset names, URLs, number of images, classes covered, and whether they overlap with our 5 classes or add new ones. Keep it concise.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, I found several pomegranate plant disease image datasets available on Roboflow Universe:

## Pomegranate Disease Dataset

The primary pomegranate disease dataset contains 906 open source images created by facedetection, with 5 object detection classes labeled A, B, C, D, and E. The dataset is licensed under CC BY 4.0.

## Fruit Disease Detection Dataset

Additionally, there is a Fruit Disease Detection Dataset by Shreya B with 244 images that includes multiple pomegranate disease classes, specifically "Pomegranate Bacterial Blight" and "Pomegranate alternaria fruit spot", along with diseases for various other fruits.

Both datasets are available on Roboflow Universe for download and use in object detection projects for agricultural applications like disease monitoring and crop management.

Based on the search results, here's what I found related to pomegranate disease datasets on these platforms:

## Pomegranate Disease Datasets

**Kaggle:** There is a Pomegranate Fruit Diseases dataset on Kaggle containing five different pomegranate fruit diseases, posted in November 2023.

**PlantVillage and HuggingFace:** The search results show several plant disease datasets on these platforms, but these appear to focus on broader crop disease detection rather than pomegranate-specific datasets:

- The PlantVillage dataset is available on HuggingFace and can be loaded with the command `dataset = load_dataset("mohanty/PlantVillage", "color")`
- The PlantVillage dataset consists of 38 classes of diseases in common crops (including healthy/normal crops)

**Recent 2024-2025 Developments:** Pomegranate fruit disease detection and classification based on computer vision remains challenging because of various diseases, making the task of collecting or creating datasets extremely difficult, according to research from 2024.

Additionally, PlantVillageVQA is a recent multimodal dataset containing 193,609 question–answer items paired with 55,448 leaf images spanning 14 crops and 38 diseases, though this appears to focus on general plant disease detection rather than specifically pomegranate.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding pomegranate disease datasets:

## Comprehensive Pomegranate Disease Datasets

The main dataset contains 5099 pomegranate fruit images labeled and classified into 5 types: Healthy, Bacterial blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora fruit spot, and Alternaria fruit spot.

Another notable dataset is the Halabja Pomegranate Fruit Disease Image Dataset, which comprises 2178 original images and 28,314 augmented images, categorized into four specific classes: ectomyelois ceratoniae, colletotrichum spp., sunburn, and healthy fruit samples.

## Disease Context

Regarding the specific diseases mentioned in your query:

- Heart rot is caused by Alternaria alternata, and fruit borer is mentioned as one of the prominent diseases of pomegranate that can be detected using deep learning approaches.

- Phytophthora palmivora has been documented as causing severe fruit rot disease of pomegranate, with lesions that rapidly enlarge superficially and internally causing partial or entire fruit rot.

These datasets are designed for developing machine learning and deep learning models for automated disease detection and classification in pomegranate crops.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the PlantVillage pomegranate disease dataset:

## PlantVillage Dataset Overview

The PlantVillage Dataset is an open access repository of 54,306 images of healthy and diseased plant leaves, collected to advance research in automated plant disease diagnosis. It covers 14 crop species and 26 diseases, making it one of the largest publicly available datasets for computer vision in agriculture.

## Accessing the Dataset

The dataset is available through multiple platforms:

1. **GitHub**: The primary source is available at spMohanty/PlantVillage-Dataset
2. **Kaggle**: The original dataset can be downloaded at https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/abdallahalidev/plantvillage-dataset
3. **Hugging Face**: The easiest way to use this dataset is via the Hugging Face Hub

## Pomegranate-Specific Data

While the search results don't specifically isolate pomegranate disease information, the dataset is comprehensive and includes more than 54,000 RGB pictures of healthy and diseased plant leaves in high quality to reduce background noise and lighting diversity across its 14 crop species.

PlantVillage offers an open-source research and educational dataset to assist research and teaching in the fields of precision agriculture, plant pathology, and vision-based crop health sensing.

Links: [{"title":"Pomegranate disease diagnosis with severity estimation and treatment remedies using deep learning and RAG-based LLM | Scientific Reports","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-25160-8"},{"title":"A Comprehensive Standardized Dataset of Numerous Pomegranate Fruit Diseases for Deep Learning","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/378671263_A_Comprehensive_Standardized_Dataset_of_Numerous_Pomegranate_Fruit_Diseases_for_Deep_Learning"},{"title":"Pomegranate disease diagnosis with severity estimation and treatment remedies using deep learning and RAG-based LLM","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC12639151/"},{"title":"Optimized deep learning framework for pomegranate disease detection using nature-inspired algorithms | Plant Methods | Full Text","url":"https://plantmethods.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s13007-025-01447-9"},{"title":"A comprehensive standardized dataset of numerous pomegranate fruit diseases for deep learning - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11220843/"},{"title":"A comprehensive standardized dataset of numerous pomegranate fruit diseases for deep learning - PubMed","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38962206/"},{"title":"Agricultural Research Communication Centre | ARCC Journals | Since 1967","url":"https://arccjournals.com/journal/indian-journal-of-agricultural-research/A-6281"},{"title":"Disease Detection & Severity Prediction of Pomegranate Fruit","url":"https://easychair.org/publications/preprint/JsH3/open"},{"title":"Performance Evaluation of Deep Learning Models for Multiclass Disease Detection in Pomegranate Fruits","url":"https://www.arccjournals.com/journal/indian-journal-of-agricultural-research/A-6396"},{"title":"Optimized deep learning framework for pomegranate disease detection using nature-inspired algorithms - PubMed","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41044589/"}]

I found several relevant research papers and datasets on pomegranate disease detection. Here are the key findings:

## Primary Dataset and Benchmarks

The most commonly used dataset contains 5099 pomegranate fruit images labeled and classified into 5 types: Healthy, Bacterial blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora fruit spot, and Alternaria fruit spot. These images were collected from July to October 2023.

## Recent Papers and Performance Benchmarks

**1. Severity Estimation with RAG-based LLM (Nature Scientific Reports, 2025)**

A dataset of 5099 annotated images was used to train several CNN models including DenseNet121, EfficientNetB0V2, MobileNetV2, ResNet50, VGG16, and InceptionV3, with DenseNet121 emerging as the top performer achieving 99.35% accuracy. The paper also developed a novel Healthy-Based Deviation Scoring method for disease severity estimation and integrated a recommendation module using a retrieval-augmented language model for treatment suggestions.

**2. PomeNetV1 Classification Model**

The PomeNetV1 model achieved 99.80% accuracy using CNN and transfer learning techniques.

**3. Nature-Inspired Optimization Framework (2025)**

A framework tested on a dataset of 5,000 images achieved 99.10% accuracy with an MLP classifier under 5-fold cross-validation and a perfect ROC-AUC score of 1.00.

**4. ResNet50-based Detection**

ResNet50 achieved superior disease detection accuracy of 97.92% on real-field pomegranate images.

The dataset is available on Mendeley Data at https://data.mendeley.com/datasets/b6s2rkpmvh/1

Links: [{"title":"Pomegranate Disease Detection Classification Dataset (v1, Pomegranate Diseases) by vehicle detection and counting","url":"https://universe.roboflow.com/vehicle-detection-and-counting-fdrtm/pomegranate-disease-detection/dataset/1"},{"title":"Performance Evaluation of Deep Learning Models for Multiclass Disease Detection in Pomegranate Fruits","url":"https://www.arccjournals.com/journal/indian-journal-of-agricultural-research/A-6396"},{"title":"Pomegranate disease diagnosis with severity estimation and treatment remedies using deep learning and RAG-based LLM | Scientific Reports","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-25160-8"},{"title":"Pomegranate Fruit Diseases Dataset for Deep Learning Models - Mendeley Data","url":"https://data.mendeley.com/datasets/b6s2rkpmvh/1"},{"title":"A comprehensive standardized dataset of numerous pomegranate fruit diseases for deep learning - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11220843/"},{"title":"A comprehensive standardized dataset of numerous pomegranate fruit diseases for deep learning","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC11220843/"},{"title":"A comprehensive standardized dataset of numerous pomegranate fruit diseases for deep learning - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2352340924002531"},{"title":"Pomegranate disease detection and classification dataset for deep learning applications: A case study from Halabja city","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC12701976/"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key findings for pomegranate disease detection datasets:

## Primary Open Source Datasets

There is a Pomegranate Disease Detection dataset containing 5099 open source disease images and annotations in multiple formats for training computer vision models, available on Roboflow.

The Pomegranate fruit diseases dataset is hosted on Mendeley Data and has been formally published in Data Brief, making it freely accessible under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License (CC BY 4.0). The dataset can be accessed directly at https://data.mendeley.com/datasets/b6s2rkpmvh.1.

## Dataset Details

The images are categorized into five types: Healthy, Bacterial blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora fruit spot, and Alternaria fruit spot resulting in a total of 5099 images. The dataset was collected during the July–October 2023 harvest season from farms in Karnataka, India, using a Redmi 9 smartphone camera, with expert annotation conducted by the Indian Institute of Horticultural Research (IIHR) in collaboration with the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR).

## Additional Datasets

There is also the Halabja Pomegranate Fruit Disease Image Dataset, which comprises 2178 original images and 28,314 augmented images, categorized into four specific classes: ectomyelois ceratoniae, colletotrichum spp., sunburn, and healthy fruit samples.

Web search results for query: "Halabja pomegranate disease dataset ectomyelois ceratoniae"

Links: [{"title":"Pomegranate disease detection and classification dataset for deep learning applications: A case study from Halabja city","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC12701976/"},{"title":"Pomegranate disease detection and classification dataset for deep learning applications: A case study from Halabja city - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2352340925010182"},{"title":"(PDF) Image Dataset of Pomegranate Fruits (Punica granatum) for various Machine Vision Applications","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/352774277_Image_Dataset_of_Pomegranate_Fruits_Punica_granatum_for_various_Machine_Vision_Applications"},{"title":"Ectomyelois ceratoniae (carob moth)","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/366189676_Ectomyelois_ceratoniae_carob_moth"},{"title":"Seasonal pattern of infestation by the carob moth Ectomyelois ceratoniae in pomegranate cultivars - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0261219417302375"},{"title":"Ectomyelois ceratoniae - an overview | ScienceDirect Topics","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/agricultural-and-biological-sciences/ectomyelois-ceratoniae"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about the Halabja pomegranate disease dataset and ectomyelois ceratoniae:

## Halabja Pomegranate Disease Dataset

The Halabja Pomegranate Fruit Disease Image Dataset is a systematically compiled collection of images from orchards in one of Iraq's major pomegranate-producing regions. The dataset comprises 2178 original images and 28,314 augmented images, categorized into four specific classes: ectomyelois ceratoniae, colletotrichum spp., sunburn, and healthy fruit samples.

Images were captured in natural outdoor environments, and a standard preprocessing step was applied which involved resizing all images to 512×512 pixels and using several image augmentation techniques to improve the flexibility and robustness of machine learning models.

## About Ectomyelois ceratoniae

The carob moth, Ectomyelois ceratoniae Zeller (Lepidoptera: Pyralidae), is an insect pest that causes damage to various crops and fruit trees throughout the world. Carob moth is a serious pest of pomegranate orchards in several parts of the world, including Turkey.

The dataset is highly suitable for developing machine learning and deep learning models aimed at plant disease detection and other computer vision tasks in precision agriculture.

content.text:
## Summary: Public Pomegranate Disease Image Datasets

Here are the publicly available pomegranate disease datasets I found, organized by source and novelty:

### **DIFFERENT SOURCES (Non-Kaggle)**

1. **Halabja Pomegranate Disease Dataset** (NEW CLASSES)
   - **Source**: ScienceDirect / PMC
   - **Images**: 2,178 original + 28,314 augmented = ~30,000 total
   - **Classes**: Ectomyelois ceratoniae (fruit borer), Colletotrichum spp., Sunburn, Healthy
   - **New diseases vs your 5-class model**: YES - includes fruit borer damage (ectomyelois ceratoniae) and sunburn which your Kaggle model doesn't have
   - **URL**: [Pomegranate disease detection and classification dataset for deep learning applications: A case study from Halabja city](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC12701976/)

2. **Mendeley Data - Pomegranate Fruit Diseases Dataset**
   - **Source**: Mendeley Data (formally published)
   - **Images**: 5,099 images
   - **Classes**: Healthy, Bacterial Blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora Fruit Spot, Alternaria Fruit Spot
   - **Overlap**: SAME 5 classes as your current Kaggle dataset (same source/researchers likely)
   - **License**: CC BY 4.0 (freely accessible)
   - **URL**: [Pomegranate Fruit Diseases Dataset for Deep Learning Models - Mendeley Data](https://data.mendeley.com/datasets/b6s2rkpmvh/1)

3. **Roboflow Universe - Pomegranate Disease**
   - **Source**: Roboflow (community-contributed)
   - **Images**: 906 open source images
   - **Classes**: 5 object detection classes (labeled A, B, C, D, E - exact disease names unclear from search)
   - **License**: CC BY 4.0
   - **URL**: [Pomegranate disease Object Detection Dataset by facedetection](https://universe.roboflow.com/facedetection-keut1/pomegranate-disease)

4. **Roboflow Universe - Fruit Disease Detection**
   - **Source**: Roboflow (Shreya B)
   - **Images**: 244 images (multi-fruit)
   - **Pomegranate classes**: Pomegranate Bacterial Blight, Pomegranate Alternaria Fruit Spot (plus other fruit diseases)
   - **Overlap**: Partial overlap with your 5 classes
   - **URL**: [Fruit Disease Detection Dataset Object Detection Dataset by Shreya B](https://universe.roboflow.com/shreya-b-sesje/fruit-disease-detection-dataset)

### **BENCHMARK DATASETS (Research Papers)**

5. **Main Comprehensive Pomegranate Dataset** (Published in Data in Brief, 2024)
   - **Images**: 5,099 fruit images
   - **Classes**: Healthy, Bacterial Blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora Fruit Spot, Alternaria Fruit Spot
   - **Collection**: July-October 2023, Karnataka India, annotated by IIHR/ICAR
   - **Benchmark papers**: 
     - [Pomegranate disease diagnosis with severity estimation and treatment remedies using deep learning and RAG-based LLM (Nature Scientific Reports, 2025)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-25160-8) - DenseNet121: 99.35% accuracy
     - [Optimized deep learning framework for pomegranate disease detection using nature-inspired algorithms (Plant Methods, 2025)](https://plantmethods.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s13007-025-01447-9)

### **GENERAL PLANT DISEASE DATASETS (May include pomegranate)**

6. **PlantVillage Dataset**
   - **Source**: Open source, multiple platforms (GitHub, HuggingFace, Kaggle, TensorFlow)
   - **Images**: 54,306 total
   - **Coverage**: 14 crop species, 26 diseases, focused on LEAVES (not fruits)
   - **Pomegranate coverage**: Likely minimal - primarily designed for leaf-based disease detection
   - **URL**: [mohanty/PlantVillage · Datasets at Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/mohanty/PlantVillage)

### **KEY FINDING**
The **Halabja dataset** is your best bet for adding NEW disease classes not in your 5-class model. It includes:
- **Ectomyelois ceratoniae** (carob moth/fruit borer damage) - NOT in your current model
- **Colletotrichum spp.** (fungal, potentially overlaps with Anthracnose but different species)
- **Sunburn** - NOT in your current model

Sources:
- [A comprehensive standardized dataset of numerous pomegranate fruit diseases for deep learning - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38962206/)
- [Pomegranate disease detection and classification dataset for deep learning applications: A case study from Halabja city](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC12701976/)
- [GitHub - spMohanty/PlantVillage-Dataset](https://github.com/spmohanty/plantvillage-dataset)
- [BrandonFors/Plant-Diseases-PlantVillage-Dataset · Datasets at Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/BrandonFors/Plant-Diseases-PlantVillage-Dataset)
- [Pomegranate Fruit Diseases [Image] Dataset | Kaggle](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset)
